# HanGuk Shin — Product Engineer · Mobile, Media & Distributed Systems

> I build complex products across mobile, media, and backend systems, designing them to converge toward the correct state even when operations fail.

Starting from Android-native apps and media processing, I've widened my scope into React Native, web, backend, and IoT systems. My strength is reproducing operational failures in real environments, then using observability and automation to reduce the risk of recurrence.

## Contact
- Email: hanguk0726@gmail.com
- GitHub: https://github.com/hanguk0726
- Medium: https://medium.com/@hangukshin
- Based in Seoul

## Core capabilities
- **Product Engineering**: 0→1 product building, Structuring requirements, App / web / back-office / backend integration, Shipping and operating, Automating a small team's repetitive work
- **Mobile**: Android / Kotlin, Jetpack Compose, React Native / Expo, Flutter, Camera · background tasks, App distribution and OTA
- **Media & Realtime**: OpenGL ES · EGL, MediaCodec, Video decode / render / encode, WebRTC, STT / TTS, Real-time audio processing
- **Backend & Distributed Systems**: Go · TypeScript · Python, PostgreSQL, MQTT · LoRa, Kafka · ClickHouse, State machines · idempotency, Retry · timeout · recovery · observability
- **Web & Visualization**: React · Next.js, WebGL, Pannellum, Back-office, Data visualization

## Featured projects

### REVITA (2025.10 – Present) — company

**A computer-vision IoT project where I built the mobile apps, public web, back-office, and field-device control backend from scratch.**

- Role: Product engineer — owned the apps (Android/iOS), web, back-office, and field-control backend end to end.
- Problems: Field devices had to be controlled remotely over an unreliable connection / Server commands and the valve's real physical state could diverge / Panorama generation was too slow to be useful on-site / A small team's repetitive work depended on individuals
- Contributions:
  - Built the product end to end on React Native, Next.js, Python, and PostgreSQL
  - Modeled valve control in a remote MQTT/LoRa device environment as a state machine, keeping state honestly pending until an ACK/NOTIFY arrives
  - Added recovery via timeouts, a safety sweep, and per-minute monitoring that nudges stalled sessions back toward correctness
  - Make-Before-Break multi-zone control to avoid deadheading a shared pump, with idempotent CAS-based pump start
  - Switched panorama generation from image stitching to video-based scanning with OpenCV
  - Automated back-office repetitive work such as GeoTIFF overlays and content into shared team workflows
  - Built tiered Sentry observability, reproduced issues on real devices, and added regression guards
- Outcomes:
  - Panorama generation time (15 min+ → ~2 min)
  - Recovered sessions that blocked the next irrigation after a lost end signal — Added a timeout and safety-sweep recovery path for stuck end_pending sessions.
  - Moved GeoTIFF and content registration into shared team workflows — Automated repetitive back-office work so the team could run it without relying on one person.
- Tech: React Native, Expo, TypeScript, Next.js, OpenCV, Pannellum, MQTT, LoRa, PostgreSQL, Airflow, Python
- Links: Medium https://medium.com/@hangukshin/%ED%95%84%EB%93%9C%EC%9D%98-%EB%B0%B8%EB%B8%8C%EB%8A%94-%EB%8D%B0%EC%9D%B4%ED%84%B0%EB%B2%A0%EC%9D%B4%EC%8A%A4-%ED%96%89%EC%9D%B4-%EC%95%84%EB%8B%88%EB%8B%A4-%EC%95%BC%EC%99%B8-%EA%B4%80%EC%88%98-%EC%8B%9C%EC%8A%A4%ED%85%9C%EC%9D%84-%EB%A7%8C%EB%93%A4%EB%A9%B0-d807466868dc

### Android Video Editor (2025.02 – 2025.06) — freelance

**An Android multi-source video editor and encoding pipeline wiring MediaCodec, OpenGL ES, and EGL.**

- Role: Android developer (project contract) — designed and built the video pipeline.
- Problems: Multiple videos and text had to be composed on one timeline / Rendered frames had to be fed back into the encoder / Needless CPU/memory copies had to be minimized / Audio and video time had to stay in sync
- Contributions:
  - Designed a Decoder → SurfaceTexture → OpenGL → Encoder pipeline
  - Frame processing with OES textures, FBOs, and shaders
  - Passed OpenGL output frames straight to the encoder through an EGLSurface
  - Timeline control over multiple video and text sources
  - Encoded PCM to AAC and muxed it into the final video
- Outcomes:
  - Removed intermediate YUV conversion — Fed OpenGL output frames to the encoder via EGLSurface, eliminating the conversion step.
  - Multi-source composition and timeline editing
- Tech: Android, Kotlin, OpenGL ES, EGL, MediaCodec, MediaMuxer
- Links: Medium (EN) https://medium.com/@hangukshin/my-first-experience-with-a-video-editor-android-app-using-opengl-7fb88765ad0d , Medium (KO) https://medium.com/@hangukshin/opengl%EC%9D%84-%ED%99%9C%EC%9A%A9%ED%95%9C-%EC%95%88%EB%93%9C%EB%A1%9C%EC%9D%B4%EB%93%9C-%EC%98%81%EC%83%81-%ED%8E%B8%EC%A7%91-%EC%95%B1-%EB%82%98%EC%9D%98-%EC%B2%AB-%EB%8F%84%EC%A0%84%EA%B8%B0-6a686ee71c0c

### Phylaxis Proxy (2023.10 – 2024.01) — company

**Built rate limiting, cache, security filtering, and the log/stats pipeline in a Go reverse proxy.**

- Role: Backend Go developer — owned rate limiting, cache, SEO, and the log/stats work.
- Problems: Request volume and resource usage had to be controlled / Malicious input had to be filtered / Cache between RAM and disk had to be managed / Large logs had to be collected and analyzed asynchronously
- Contributions:
  - Rate limiting and an LRU RAM/disk cache with TTL management
  - Parsed request headers/query/body and analyzed input to defend against SQLi and XSS
  - Log and statistics processing with Kafka and ClickHouse
  - HAProxy integration (certs, fingerprint, load balancing) and Docker packaging and deploy
- Tech: Go, Aerospike, ClickHouse, Kafka, HAProxy, Docker
- Links: phylaxis.net https://phylaxis.net/

### Togispace (2022.01 – 2023.02) — company

**Built a commercial Android app from scratch and operated it for 14 months through repeated product pivots.**

- Role: Android developer — sole owner of the client through build and operations.
- Problems: The app's structure had to stay stable through fast pivots / Complex UI (gestures, nested scroll) and state management / Long-term operation and UX stability
- Contributions:
  - Built the app on Kotlin and Jetpack Compose
  - Clean Architecture and a multi-module structure with event-driven, reactive programming
  - Auth, camera, PDF, WebView, i18n, and network/image caching
  - Complex widgets (calendar, cropper, swipe, drag-and-drop) and app state management
  - Collaborated with other roles across the full product lifecycle
- Outcomes:
  - 14 months operating a B2C app
- Tech: Kotlin, Jetpack Compose

### Avatar Vision (2023.02 – 2023.05) — personal

**A video-diary app in Flutter and Rust without ffmpeg — decoding webcam/mic input and muxing to MP4.**

- Role: Personal project — designed and built the whole Flutter desktop UI and Rust media pipeline (Windows).
- Problems: Webcam and mic input had to be saved as MP4 without a heavy dependency like ffmpeg / Live mirroring and encoding had to share one frame stream without slowing each other down / Webcam frame formats differed by device (MJPEG, NV12, YUV) / MP4 needs an AAC audio track, but the Rust-ported MP4 muxer had no audio support
- Contributions:
  - Captured the webcam with nokhwa and mic PCM with cpal, wiring Flutter and Rust over irondash message channels and a native texture for the live preview
  - Mirroring decoded frames in a parallel thread pool and rendered only the newest (dropping late frames); encoding batched frames to 24fps by comparing timestamps
  - Converted RGBA→YUV (I420) and encoded H.264 with openh264 — an index-ordered queue kept frame order across parallel workers, scaling them 2→8 once the preview shut off
  - Read the original C muxer to wire an audio track into the Rust-ported minimp4, encoding PCM to AAC with fdk-aac and muxing it with the H.264 into MP4
  - Handled per-device frame formats — MJPEG/NV12 on a Logitech C922, YUV on a ThinkPad's embedded camera
  - Desktop UX with a custom-painter sound-wave visual, a from-scratch player UI on media_kit, thumbnails extracted during encoding, and ObjectBox storage
- Outcomes:
  - Capture → encode → mux fully covered by a self-built pipeline, no ffmpeg — Completed video-diary saving with nokhwa, cpal, openh264, fdk-aac, and minimp4 alone.
  - Fed the fixes back upstream as two PRs to minimp4.rs and nokhwa — Shared the audio-muxing and camera-capture solutions for others hitting the same walls.
- Tech: Flutter, Dart, Rust, OpenH264, fdk-aac, minimp4, irondash, nokhwa, cpal
- Links: GitHub https://github.com/hanguk0726/Avatar-Vision , Medium https://medium.com/@hangukshin/building-a-video-diary-app-written-in-flutter-and-rust-without-ffmpeg-8eb97a97fb49

## Experience

- **REVITA** — Product Engineer · Yongsan, Seoul · 2025.10 – Present — React Native Android & iOS apps; Next.js web and back-office; MQTT/LoRa-based IoT control; Observability and operations automation
- **Freelance** — Android Developer · Project contract · 2025.02 – 2025.06 — Designed and built the decode/compose/encode pipeline; Passed render frames to the encoder via EGLSurface
- **Phylaxis** — Backend Go Developer · Jung-gu, Seoul · 2023.10 – 2024.01 — Rate limit and LRU cache / TTL management; Input analysis for SQLi/XSS defense; Kafka/ClickHouse logs and statistics
- **Togispace** — Android Developer · Yeouido, Seoul · 2022.01 – 2023.02 — Built a Clean Architecture, multi-module structure; Auth, camera, PDF, WebView, and i18n; Improved app state management through repeated pivots
- **Snaptag** — Android & Backend Developer · Pangyo, Gyeonggi · 2021.03 – 2021.06 — Optimized from camera capture to information extraction; Passed camera frames to a C++ module via JNI

## Project archive
- **VocalBridge** (2025.08 – 2025.08) — A prototype linking real-time voice through STT, translation, and TTS over React Native and WebRTC.
- **CrowdFunding** (2024.01 – 2024.03) — Crowdfunding on a smart contract — campaign creation, funding, and claim, syncing frontend CRUD with on-chain transactions.
- **Realtime Chat** (2021.01 – 2021.02) — Multi-channel real-time messaging with a websocket pool, read receipts, and magic-link login.
- **Plantimer** (2021.09 – 2021.10) — A productivity app that schedules tasks as a progress-bar timer, continuing via notifications when closed.
- **Strawberry Days** (2020.11 – 2020.12) — A fan site for singer Younha — aggregated social updates, album/track browsing, and community.
- **Snaptag** (2021.03 – 2021.06) — Scanning invisible QR codes with the Android camera — passing frames to a C++ module via JNI and optimizing capture-to-extraction.
- **Openshy4J** (2020.09 – 2020.09) — An OpenStack web controller to create and manage instances via Spring and openstack4j.
- **Fivestar Recipe** (2020.02 – 2020.03) — A cooking-recipe site with bookmarks, search, and ranking (Spring MVC, Vue.js).
- **Blue Marble** (2019.12 – 2019.12) — My first project after learning Java — Monopoly-style board-game logic in the terminal.

## Interested in
- Products where users interact with AI over the long term
- Multimodal tools combining video, image, and voice
- Real-time communication and media processing
- Services where mobile is the core interface
- Systems that connect real-world hardware with software
- Distributed systems with failure, delay, and duplication
- Turning a small team's repetitive work into product and automation

Good-fit roles: Product Engineer, Mobile Engineer, Android Engineer, Multimodal / Media Engineer, Full-stack Product Engineer, Founding Engineer.

I prefer environments where I can own the user problem and the whole system flow, rather than roles narrowly scoped to isolated feature work.
